text: The laparoscopic camera provides a view of the peritoneum as the visualized trocar port enters the abdomen. The surgeon performs diagnostic laparoscopy to identify a single adhesion band extending from the transverse colon to the mesentery, causing obstruction at the proximal ileum.

The surgeon performs sharp dissection using scissors to safely lyse the adhesion band between the transverse colon and mesentery, avoiding heat effects on the bowel. The final step involves inspecting the entire intestine from the duodenal junction to the ileocecal valve.

During the diagnostic laparoscopy, multiple adhesion bands are identified between the omentum and the abdominal wall. The surgeon uses cold scissors to safely excise these bands, while optimal retraction of the bowel is maintained using an atraumatic grasper.
